message:
fix(math): saturate all u64/i16/Duration conversions along the retry + toast paths

Three related numeric-edge-case fixes, grouped because they all
replace "cast-and-hope" arithmetic with explicit saturation so that
extreme inputs (very large backoff parameters, u16::MAX toast
dimensions, enormous retry counts) no longer wrap, panic under
overflow checks, or produce silently-wrong delays.

1) crates/doctor_frankentui/src/effect_translator.rs

   compute_retry_delay() had two overflow foot-guns:

   - Exponential branch used `multiplier.powi(attempt as i32)`.
     `powi` is safe for f64 but the surrounding `delay.round() as u64`
     saturates via the standard float-to-int semantics only since
     the 2022 as-cast change; more importantly `powi(i32)` forced a
     lossy u32→i32 conversion that flipped sign for attempt >= 2^31.
     Switched to `powf(f64::from(attempt))` and routed the result
     through a new `round_nonnegative_f64_to_u64_saturating` helper
     that pins NaN/negative inputs to 0 and anything >= u64::MAX
     to u64::MAX before rounding.
   - Linear branch was `base_ms * (attempt as u64 + 1)` — a plain
     multiply that overflows for any non-trivial base with large
     attempts (debug panic, release wraparound). Rewrote as
     `base_ms.saturating_mul(u64::from(attempt).saturating_add(1))`.
   - Also corrected the `Linear` doc comment, which described the
     formula as `base_ms * attempt` when the implementation (and
     the tests) have always used `base_ms * (attempt + 1)`.
   - Added `linear_backoff_saturates_on_overflow` and an
     exponential-overflow regression test that lock the saturation
     behavior in.

2) crates/ftui-runtime/src/retry.rs

   `RetryPolicy::total_max_delay()` summed per-attempt delays in a
   plain `Duration += Duration` loop. With max_retries in the
   millions (not hypothetical — this gets fed into external budget
   calculations) the loop was both slow and capable of saturating
   Duration internally with no caller-visible signal.

   Replaced with a closed-form implementation per backoff variant:

   - Fixed: a single saturating multiplication of delay_ms by
     retry_count.
   - Linear: compute how many terms fit under the per-delay `max_ms`
     cap as `uncapped_terms = min(retry_count, max_ms / base_ms)`,
     use the arithmetic-series identity
     `sum = n * (n + 1) / 2` for those, then add
     `(retry_count - uncapped_terms) * max_ms` for the remainder.
     All arithmetic is u128 with `.saturating_*` at every step and
     a final clamp to `Duration::MAX.as_millis()` before converting
     back.
   - Exponential: walk attempts with `1_u64.checked_shl(attempt)`
     (defaulting to max_ms on overflow). Once the delay saturates
     at max_ms, short-circuit the remainder as
     `remaining * max_ms` in one saturating multiplication instead
     of looping.

   Added two new helpers — `duration_from_millis_saturating(u128)`
   and `add_millis_saturating(&mut u128, u128)` — that centralize
   the Duration conversion, guarding against u64/u32 overflow on
   the seconds/nanos fields. New tests lock in the Fixed-saturation
   fast path (no-iteration proof is implicit in the test not
   timing out) and the boundary where Linear or Exponential should
   switch to max_ms-dominated sums.

3) crates/ftui-widgets/src/toast.rs

   Toast entrance/exit animations were converting `u16` dimensions
   to `i16` via raw `as i16` casts in four places, each of which
   wraps to a large negative number at toast_width/height > i16::MAX
   (65535 fits u16 fine, 32768+ is UB-adjacent when cast-and-negated
   to i16). For a widget that in theory receives viewport-scoped
   dimensions this was probably unreachable in practice, but it's
   cheap to fix and expensive to debug later.

   Extracted `ToastEntranceAnimation::offset_from_dimension(u16) ->
   i16` that saturates to i16::MAX via `TryFrom` and fed every
   SlideFromTop/Right/Bottom/Left (entrance) and
   SlideToTop/Right/Bottom/Left (exit) site through it. The Slide*
   arms negate the saturated value as needed so the left/top
   directions produce -i16::MAX rather than wrapping.
   entrance_offsets_saturate_large_dimensions and
   exit_offsets_saturate_large_dimensions pin the new behavior
   with u16::MAX inputs.

No behavior change for any realistic input; only the pathological
tails are now deterministic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
